t_ch: 不必再哀嚎悲泣，我将为你…带去安息。
t_en: There's no more need to weep in grief. I... will grant you peace.

t_ch: 曾经拯救我的少女…你真的业已消散了吗？
t_en: Is the girl who once saved my life... truly gone?

t_ch: 又或者，在遗恨消去后…将能再度示现？
t_en: Or will you show yourself again... once we eradicate this hatred and regret?

t_ch: 小心，尽管只出于本能，但它正积蓄着盛怒——
t_en: Be careful. They may be moving by instinct, but there is fury welling up within them —

t_ch: 姐…姐……
t_en: Sis... ter...

t_ch: 嗯…我回来了。
t_en: Yes, it's me. I'm back.

t_ch: 求你…别向冥界…回头……
t_en: Please... don't... turn back... to... the nether realm...

t_ch: 对不起，我不得不将你从远古的死亡中唤醒……
t_en: I'm sorry I have to wake you up from ancient death...

t_ch: 我明白，你很不安…别害怕……
t_en: And I know you're anxious... Shh, don't be afraid...

t_ch: *悲伤的低鸣*
还记得我吗？我就是你曾经护送过的，那个横渡冥海的女孩。
t_en: Do you remember me? I'm the girl you once carried across the Sea of Souls.

t_ch: 对不起，出于一些原因，我的记忆中没能留下你的身影。但在许多人的帮助下，我终于得以拾起那些旧日的碎片……
t_en: I'm sorry I wasn't able to remember you, but with the help of many people, I was finally able to pick up these broken fragments of my past...

t_ch: …玻吕刻斯，我回来了。
t_en: ...I'm back, Pollux.

t_ch: 虽然我知道，这也许不是你期待的。
t_en: I know this might not be what you wanted.

t_ch: *悲伤的低鸣*
但是，我必须这么做。
t_en: But I have to do this.

t_ch: 所以现在，请带领我渡过冥海，回到冥界，就像你曾经做的那样……
t_en: So please, carry me across the Sea of Souls again and return me to the nether realm, just like you did in the past...

t_ch: 带我去往那「死亡」火种的长眠之地，好吗？
t_en: Please take me to where the Coreflame of "Death" rests.

t_ch: …二位。我要出发了。
t_en: ...I'll be going now.

t_ch: 太好了，我们出发吧。
t_en: Great, let's go.

t_ch: 我们…不会要钻进蛋里吧…
t_en: Uh... We won't have to go into an egg, right?

t_ch: 虽然它现在看上去有些可怕，但其实…我们…只要……
t_en: They may look scary, but as long... as... we...

t_ch: （这是…怎么了……）
t_en: (What's... happening...)

t_ch: …伙伴…怎么…？！
t_en: Huh...? What's wrong...!?

t_ch: 头好晕…
t_en: I feel dizzy...

t_ch: 我好像…
t_en: I think I...

t_ch: 要不行了…
t_en: I'm done for...

t_ch: 阁下…？
t_en: Are you...?

t_ch: 伙伴{M#他}{F#她}…这是怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong with {M#him?}{F#her?}

t_ch: 看来时间快到了。如果再不动身，恐怕…{M#他}{F#她}的灵魂会先我一步回归{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的怀抱。
t_en: {M#His}{F#Her} time is almost up. If I don't go now, I'm afraid {M#his}{F#her} soul will return to {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' embrace before me.

t_ch: 这…怎、怎么办？还能抢救一下吗？
t_en: What! Oh no... Is there anything we can do?

t_ch: 别担心，我承诺过：我会代阁下向{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}讨回行走大地的权利。
t_en: Don't worry. I promised I would ask {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} for the Trailblazer's right to roam the earth again.

t_ch: 迷迷，你在这里尽可能照看好阁下，等我回来。好吗？
t_en: Mem, stay here and keep a close watch until I return, alright?

t_ch: 没问题！人家最擅长的就是关心照顾别人了。
t_en: Alright! I'm the best at taking care of others.

t_ch: 去吧，小蝶。你也要记得…别给自己留下遗憾哪。
t_en: Go on, Cas. Make sure not to leave yourself with any regrets.

t_ch: 别留遗憾…
t_en: No regrets...

t_ch: 慢慢来，我还能撑住…
t_en: Take your time. I can still hold out...

t_ch: 我也出发，看咱们谁先到冥界…
t_en: I'll go too. Let's see who gets to the nether realm first...

t_ch: 这种时候就别开玩笑了！
t_en: That's not funny!

t_ch: 那，我们约好了。
t_en: It's a deal then.

t_ch: 阁下……
t_en: Please...

t_ch: …等我。
t_en: Wait for me.

t_ch: 哦，你们坐在你们的小小船上\n渴求聆听我的歌唱而紧随其后\n请你们且回头，回到你们自己熟悉的故土
t_en: Oh, you sit in your little dinghy\nEager to hear my song and follow closely behind\nPlease, turn back, return to the homeland you know so well

t_ch: 不要随我冒险驶向茫茫的大海，说不定\n见不到我，你们会在大海中迷途\n我横渡的大洋从来没有人曾航行过
t_en: Venture not with me into the vast sea, for perhaps\nWithout seeing me, you will lose your way in the ocean\nThe ocean I cross has never been sailed by anyone

t_ch: 最后……
t_en: After all that...

t_ch: 你还是回到了这里。
t_en: You still ended up back here.

t_ch: 冥界…果真如传说那样，是一片花海。
t_en: So the legends were true. The nether realm really is a sea of flowers.

t_ch: 是呀。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 就像…我们从前照料的那片花田。
t_en: Just like the flower fields we tended to back then...

t_ch: 是你吗…玻吕茜亚？
t_en: Is that you... Polyxia?

t_ch: 你还记得我吗？
t_en: Do you remember me?

t_ch: …我没有那段记忆，只是在一场实验中目睹过你的身影。
t_en: ...I don't have the memories from that time, but I've seen you before during an experiment.

t_ch: 这样呀。
t_en: I see.

t_ch: 那，还是叫我{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}吧。这样更轻松些。
t_en: Call me {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} then. It'll be easier.

t_ch: 不过，既然知道那个名字，就代表你已经知晓有关「{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}」的全部了吧？
t_en: However, since you recognize the name, you must already know everything about {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, right?
Yes....

t_ch: 我…为重铸翁法罗斯生死秩序而来。
t_en: I'm... here to reforge the order of life and death in Amphoreus.

t_ch: 你知道吗？我的姐姐，是个善解人意、又有些敏感的人。每当她有心事，却又说不出口的时候，就会躲避别人的视线，望向远方……
t_en: You know, my sister was a sympathetic but rather sensitive person. Whenever she didn't want to say what was on her mind, she would avoid eye contact and look off into the distance...

t_ch: 就像现在这样。
t_en: Just like what you are doing now.

t_ch: 可以告诉我吗？上一次「再创世」……
t_en: Can you tell me... about the last Era Nova?

t_ch: 玻吕茜亚…究竟做了什么？
t_en: What did you do, Polyxia?

t_ch: 那，该从哪里说起呢？不妨就从最初开始吧……
t_en: Where should I begin...? I suppose we can start from the beginning.

t_ch: 好了，我亲爱的玻吕茜亚……
t_en: Alright, my dear Polyxia...

t_ch: 取出我的心脏，点燃「死亡」的火苗——
t_en: It's time to extract my heart and light the flame of Death...

t_ch: 然后，用它在预言许诺的新世界里，{RUBY_B#带去生命}播下第一枚花蕾{RUBY_E#}吧。
t_en: After that, use it to {RUBY_B#bring life}plant the first bud{RUBY_E#} in the prophesied new world.

t_ch: 在久远的过去，有一对同心的双子。根据预言，她们被选中成为「死亡」之神的递补。
t_en: A long, long time ago, there were a pair of like-minded twins. As foretold in the prophecy, they were chosen to be successors to the god of "Death."

t_ch: 可命运弄人，作为成神的代价<color=#dbc291ff>…我们必须亲手夺去至亲的生命。</color>
t_en: But fate was cruel. The price of attaining divinity was to <color=#dbc291ff>take the life of our closest kin with our own hands.</color>

t_ch: 这场试炼…也记录在了那段记忆中。
t_en: That trial... was indeed recorded in that memory.

t_ch: 那你应该知道，是姐姐主动献出了自己的生命，将我送上「死亡」半神的宝座。
t_en: Then you should already know that my sister voluntarily gave up her own life so that I could ascend as the demigod of "Death."

t_ch: 但…我无法接受这一切。为了一个她无法见证的未来，我们不得不自相残杀…这实在太不公平。
t_en: But I couldn't accept that. Why did we have to kill each other for a future she'd never see herself? That's not fair.

t_ch: 所以，在编织新世界的法则时，我…打破了「生」与「死」的底线。
t_en: So, while weaving the laws of the new world... I shattered the forbidden bottom line for "life" and "death."

t_ch: 我将她的灵魂捏塑成型，又以巨龙玻吕刻斯的模样，承载着姐姐在冥河里溯流而上，送她重返人间。
t_en: I reshaped my sister's soul and took the form of the great dragon Pollux to carry her up the River of Souls and back into the mortal realm.

t_ch: 可惜命运早已注定…在我徒劳的尝试下，它以意想不到的形式兑现了。
t_en: But our fates were already decided... and manifested in unexpected ways in response to my futile actions.

t_ch: 生老病死乃是自然颠扑不破的法则，它随「死亡」诞生一同写就。生者离世，其灵魂将觐见{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}，并由后者裁决将去往何处……
t_en: Aging is an unbreakable law of nature laid upon humankind, created at the same time as "Death." When the living die, their souls will meet {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, who would determine where every soul goes...

t_ch: 若死神举起左手，则灵魂受赐死亡，步入冥界，等待转生；若举起右手，则灵魂将被冥界拒绝，回归人间，继续在大地上前进。
t_en: If the Reaper raises their left hand, the soul is granted death and allowed to enter the nether realm to await reincarnation. If they raise their right hand, the soul is rejected by the nether realm and returned to the mortal realm to continue walking the earth.

t_ch: 所以…{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}「灰黯之手」{RUBY_E#}只能施行宣判，而不能进行拥抱。
t_en: That's why the {RUBY_B#Death Titan}"Hand of Shadow"{RUBY_E#} can only lay down judgment... and not embrace another being.

t_ch: 是啊。正如「死亡」本为一体的双掌，在我们抵达人间时一分为二，再也无法触及对方……
t_en: Yes. "Death" was originally a pair of hands, but they were split in twain when we reached the mortal realm, never to touch each other ever again...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的生之半身，「赐予死亡」的永生之人…这就是我强加于你的诅咒。
t_en: The half of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} that represents life, the undying human who "grants death"... That was the curse I forced upon you.

t_ch: 那…你呢？
t_en: Then... What about you?

t_ch: 它的死之半身，我背负「拒绝死亡」的命运，在人间诞生的那一瞬间便宣告死去。
t_en: As the half of Thanatos representing death, I shoulder the fate of "defying death." I died the moment I entered the world of the living.

t_ch: 自此，泰坦也在世上不复存在。生死断绝流转，灵魂无缘面见尊神，只能搁浅在亡者有涨无落的河流中……
t_en: From then on, my Titan also no longer existed in this world. The cycle of life and death stopped. Souls were denied their audience with death, and were left stranded within an ever-rising river of the dead....

t_ch: 多可笑啊…我的私心本为挽救生命，却为你，还有这个世界写下了比死亡更沉重的命运。
t_en: What irony. My selfish desire to save your life gave you and the world a fate far worse than death instead.

t_ch: …命运果然很荒诞。
t_en: ...Fate is absurd indeed.

t_ch: 我们的人生如同手掌对映…可即便如此，仍旧殊途同归——
t_en: Our lives were a mirrored image of one another, like a pair of hands... Even so, we arrived at the same destination.

t_ch: 我们走过不同的路，却品尝着同样的滋味。
t_en: We walked different paths, yet experienced the same pain.

t_ch: 我本以为，至少你能在这一世好好活下去……
t_en: I had hoped that maybe you would at least have a good life in this cycle...

t_ch: …对不起。
t_en: ...I'm sorry.

t_ch: 我的人生并没有虚度。对我而言，你不曾做错过什么。
t_en: I lived my life well. It didn't go to waste. As far as I'm concerned, you did nothing wrong.

t_ch: 即便…我将「赐死」的权柄强加给与我素不相识的你？
t_en: Even though I forced the authority of "granting death to humanity" upon you, a total stranger?

t_ch: 在这场漫长的旅途中，我的肩头早已被强加过太多使命。我也曾迷茫，乃至愠怒过……
t_en: Too many responsibilities were forced upon me in the course of this long journey. I was once lost, even upset...

t_ch: 但直到不久前，我才意识到：若非你的赐福，我也无法理解那些感情从何而来，无法理解自己的使命——
t_en: But I realized recently that if it weren't for your blessing, I wouldn't have understood the source of these emotions, nor would I have realized my purpose...

t_ch: 更遑论行至此地，与我宿命的半身重逢；一同分享我们的过去，眼前的风景……
t_en: I wouldn't have made it here to reunite with my missing half, and we wouldn't be discussing our past while sharing the same view of this landscape...

t_ch: 还有，你我二人「存在」的意义。
t_en: I wouldn't have understood the meaning of our "existence."

t_ch: 存在的意义……
t_en: The meaning of our existence...

t_ch: 这片花海生长在死地，你我用离别为它播种，又以孤独的泪水浇灌而成…是啊，「死亡」所能缔造的，莫过于血泪中绽放的鲜花而已。
t_en: This sea of flowers blooms on dead land because we sowed it with the seeds of our separation and watered it with our tears of loneliness... Yes, the only thing "Death" is allowed to create are flowers that bloom from blood and tears.

t_ch: 所以，{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}并不能随心所欲地塑造万物，{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}「灰黯之手」{RUBY_E#}也无法裁夺万物生灵的命运——
t_en: That's why {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} cannot create living beings at will, nor can the {RUBY_B#Death Titan}"Hand of Shadow"{RUBY_E#} determine a person's remaining days...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>因为凡人的生与死，本就不该被任何意志主宰。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Because the life and death of mortals should never be dictated by another's will.</color>

t_ch: 这听起来，反而是在否定{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的意义……
t_en: Are you saying there's no point in {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' existence...?

t_ch: 不。无人能独力担起生死的天平，哪怕是神明。
t_en: No. No single being can bear the scales of life and death on their own, not even a god.

t_ch: 生与死的分量同样沉重…你我都再明白不过了，不是么？
t_en: Life and death weigh equally heavy... We both know that now, don't we?

t_ch: 你这一生，想必也很辛苦吧……
t_en: You, too, must have had a difficult life...

t_ch: 我们…真是一同走过了无比荒谬的旅途啊。
t_en: We've taken quite the ridiculous journey together, haven't we?

t_ch: 已经太久太久了。我一个人守候在这里，日复一日地望着走投无路的亡灵被这片孤独的荒原拒之门外……
t_en: It's been far too long. I've waited here for centuries, watching day after day as desperate spirits are denied by this lonely wasteland...

t_ch: 在他们中，我始终期待着看到你的身影，却又害怕和你相认。我不知道要如何面对你……
t_en: I always hoped I would see you among them, but I was also afraid of seeing you. I didn't know how I could possibly face you again...

t_ch: 我更害怕看见…曾经的至亲早已忘却了我的存在。
t_en: But... more than anything, I was afraid that you might have forgotten all about me.

t_ch: …我明白。但你看，玻吕茜亚，无论如何…我回到了这里。
t_en: ...I know. But don't you see, Polyxia? I made it back anyway.

t_ch: 你不必再独自肩负「死亡」的权柄…毕竟，它本就应该由两只手共同举起。
t_en: You don't have to shoulder Death's authority on your own anymore... It was always meant to be held in two hands anyway.

t_ch: 所以，就让{NICKNAME}带着我们的火种回到人间，让凡人取回生死流转的权利……
t_en: So, let's give our Coreflame to {NICKNAME} and have {M#him}{F#her} take it back to the mortal realm. Give humanity back their cycle of life and death.

t_ch: 我们，{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的职责，从来都不是拣选生命，只有看顾它们而已。
t_en: Our responsibility as {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} was never about picking out favorite souls. We were only meant to watch over life.

t_ch: 让我们一起…为众多灵魂照料这片属于他们的、温暖的花海吧。
t_en: Let's tend the field together and create a warm sea of flowers for all the souls to come.

t_ch: 好吗，玻吕茜亚？
t_en: What do you think, Polyxia?

t_ch: 当然。现在…也只差最后一步了。
t_en: Yes. Just... one last step.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「汝将凋零，令逝者自残余中发芽，一同死去的火新生」……</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"You shall wither, and through that, the dead will sprout again from the remains, and be reborn with the dead flame"...</color>

t_ch: 如神谕所示，塞纳托斯的神权注定只能由一人执掌。身为「死亡」的黄金裔，身为它的半神，身为泰坦……
t_en: As foretold by the prophecy, Thanatos' authority is only destined for only one person to wield. As the Chrysos Heir of "Death," as their demigod, as a Titan...

t_ch: …我们命中注定的时刻，再次到来了。
t_en: ...Our fated moment has arrived once again.

t_ch: 就像从前那样……
t_en: Just like before...

t_ch: 是啊，就像从前。
t_en: Yes, just like before.

t_ch: 但，与那次不同的是…这一次，试炼的双方都做好了准备。
t_en: But unlike that time, the ones undergoing the trial this time are prepared.

t_ch: 你已经…下定决心了？
t_en: You've... made up your mind?

t_ch: 嗯…此乃命运使然。但只要有你在，我就不会害怕。
t_en: Yes... as dictated by fate. But I won't be afraid, as long as you're here with me.

t_ch: 请你完成这一世的试炼，赐我以「死亡」的拥抱，点燃「生命」的火苗。
t_en: Complete your trial in this life. Grant me the embrace of "Death" and light the spark of "Life" ablaze.

t_ch: 如果是你，在经历过生与死的锤炼之后，一定能为这片荒原带来第一抹生机……
t_en: You've been tempered by life and death. I know you'll be able to give this wasteland its first breath of life...

t_ch: 就像奔赴冥界的蝴蝶，落在死亡的枝头。
t_en: Like a raven butterfly flying swiftly into the nether realm, perching on the twig of death.

t_ch: …我会的。在那之后，我会永居冥界，让冥河回到这里，化作天上的细雨，将灵魂播撒向这片冥土……
t_en: ...I will. After this, I will remain permanently in the nether realm. The River of Souls shall return here and rain down from the sky, scattering souls across the earth...

t_ch: 生死将再度流转。你亲手播下的这片花海，今后将承载无数或悲或喜的灵魂，永远焕发生机。
t_en: The cycle of life and death will flow once again. This sea of flowers you sowed shall bear countless joyous or sorrowful souls, and bloom evermore.

t_ch: …谢谢你。
t_en: ...Thank you.

t_ch: 最后的最后，能请你…为我实现一个心愿么？
t_en: Just one last thing. Can you... grant me one wish?

t_ch: 当然可以。是什么？
t_en: Of course. What is it?

t_ch: 请…不要忘记我。
t_en: Please... don't forget me.

t_ch: …我答应你。
t_en: ...I promise you.

t_ch: 即便我将再度跨越轮回…当我们在来世再见时，我也一定会像最初诞生时那样……
t_en: When we meet in the next world... Even if I have endured another reincarnation...

t_ch: 即便，我们尚不知道对方的名字……
t_en: ...Even if we do not know each other's names...

t_ch: …我也一定会紧紧地、紧紧地抱住你。
t_en: ...I will still hold you tightly in my arms, just like what I did when we were born.

t_ch: 遐蝶…遐蝶……
t_en: Castorice... Castorice...

t_ch: 人如其名…多美呀。
t_en: You're as beautiful... as a fleeting butterfly.

t_ch: 回头吧…回头吧……
t_en: Go back whence you came... Go back...

t_ch: 前方是那死者的疆域哪……
t_en: Ahead lies the realm of the dead...

t_ch: 这些人影…是冥河倒映出的幻象吗？
t_en: These shadowy figures... are they illusions reflected by the River of Souls?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，别害怕，和我一起…睁大眼睛！
t_en: Don't be afraid, {NICKNAME}. Let's open our eyes wide... together!

t_ch: 奇怪，要说是冥河的倒影，似乎又太过…灵动。
t_en: Strange... For reflections in the River of Souls, they seem oddly... animated.

t_ch: 来，这边这边！
t_en: Hey, over here!

t_ch: 搞定！这样就安全啦。
t_en: Done! Now it'll be safe for us to move on.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的奇迹，显现了……
t_en: {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s miracle has appeared...

t_ch: 好神奇！就像待在一个巨大的泡泡里，河水都被分开了。
t_en: Amazing! It's like we're inside a giant bubble. Even the river got parted.

t_ch: 岁月将河水分割为了「过去」和「现在」……
t_en: Time has split the river into "the past" and "the present"...

t_ch: 要是丹恒在，冥河水肯定能升天吧……
t_en: If Dan Heng was around, he'd definitely be able to raise the waters of the River of Souls into the sky...

t_ch: 如果海瑟音大人还在世，她应该也能做到。
t_en: If Lady Hysilens was still alive, I think she might be able to do that, too.

t_ch: 啊…以前这条路反而是走不通的吗？
t_en: Huh... So this path actually didn't exist back then?

t_ch: 这里…有锁链生锈的味道。
t_en: There's... the scent of iron rust over here.

t_ch: 有人来了，准备——
t_en: Someone's coming, ready...

t_ch: 整齐！列队！
t_en: Formation!

t_ch: 看！世间果真有粉色的小犬！
t_en: Look! Pink puppies do exist!

t_ch: 前路又被冥河截断了……
t_en: The path ahead is blocked by the River of Souls again...

t_ch: 那我们再回到「过去」吧！不过，这神迹也太方便了……
t_en: In that case, let us go back to "the past"! This miracle sure is handy...

t_ch: …这也是泰坦神力的一道侧面。
t_en: ...This is yet another aspect of a Titan's divine power.

t_ch: 这里是过不去的，我们得返回「现在」。
t_en: We can't get through here. Let us return to "the present."

t_ch: 呀…这样的话，反而没有路能走了。
t_en: Uh... If you do that, we'll actually run into a dead end.

t_ch: 那里的「预言算碑」…也许能派上用途？
t_en: Perhaps the Prophecy Tablet over there can help...?

t_ch: 阁下的眼睛，似乎能看清过去和未来……
t_en: You seem to be able to discern both the past and the future...

t_ch: 是…你们啊……
t_en: It's... you people...

t_ch: 别别别害怕，这些亡魂根本就没发现我们。
t_en: D—D—Don't be scared. They haven't noticed us at all.

t_ch: 这里…以前肯定是座很美的庭院。
t_en: This place... must have been a beautiful courtyard in the past.

t_ch: 但是，该怎么上去呢？
t_en: But how should we get up there?

t_ch: 路又堵住了。
t_en: The path is blocked yet again.

t_ch: 这种时候，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的助力总会恰到好处地出现呢……
t_en: {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s aid always appears in times like this. How convenient...

t_ch: 看，那里还有升降平台。
t_en: Look. There's even a platform lift over there.

t_ch: 还真是被恰到好处地藏起来了…这是扎格列斯的恶作剧吗？
t_en: Look at how well-concealed this is... Is this one of Zagreus' pranks?

t_ch: 「过去的宝箱」被留了下来，可里面的财宝却没有留下……
t_en: A "Chest of the Past" has been left behind, but none of its treasures remain inside...

t_ch: （我哭了，真的。）
t_en: (I'm crying!)

t_ch: 等等！要是奇迹突然失效怎么办？
t_en: W—Wait! What if the miracle suddenly loses its effect?

t_ch: 迷迷…你真的要我说出来吗？
t_en: Mem... Do you really want me to answer that?

t_ch: 别过来…别过来！
t_en: Stay back... Don't come any closer!

t_ch: *远方传来的轰鸣*
t_en: *A rumble from the distance*

t_ch: 这、这是……
t_en: This... This is...

t_ch: …生者？
t_en: ...Living ones?
"Strife"... "Protection"...

t_ch: 那边便是「高塔」……
t_en: The "tower" is right over there...

t_ch: 迷迷成功了……
t_en: Mem did it...

t_ch: 粉色松鼠…很机灵。
t_en: The pink squirrel... is very smart.

t_ch: …人家怎么又变成松鼠了？
t_en: ...Why am I a squirrel now?

t_ch: 奇迹作用的范围不大，还请阁下再靠近一些……
t_en: The miracle's range is limited. Please come a little closer...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，人家果然也适合当{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}祭司吧？
t_en: Heehee. Aren't I a good fit for {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s priest?

t_ch: 当然，你是最称职的祭司。
t_en: Of course, you're the most competent priest ever.

t_ch: 这扇大门之后…应该就是炼金法阵所在了。
t_en: The alchemical matrix... should be behind this door.

t_ch: 那是……
t_en: Is that...

t_ch: 就让仪式…开始吧。
t_en: Then... let the ritual begin.

t_ch: 然后，我，玻吕茜亚残存的理智，徒留在冥界……
t_en: Then, what was left of my reason was left behind in the nether realm...

t_ch: 日复一日地照料鲜花，看它长成花海……
t_en: Where I've been taking care of these flowers day after day, until there is now a sea of them....

t_ch: …咀嚼着生与死，离别，还有漫长的孤独。
t_en: ...All while mulling over the subject of life, death, partings, and enduring an endless loneliness.

t_ch: 早在那时，我已有预感：总有一天，我们还会再相见……
t_en: I had a feeling even back then that we would meet again one day...

t_ch: 不为久别重逢的拥抱，而为以手还手，实现报应……
t_en: Not to embrace one another after a long separation, but to come in search of retribution.

t_ch: 告诉我早已写就的答案…为漫长的命运画上句号。
t_en: Tell me the answer that's already been written... and let us draw this seemingly endless fate to a close.

t_ch: 啊…为何没有一位神明在远方高喊……
t_en: Ah... Why isn't there a god yelling in the distance...

t_ch: 「是呀，受苦受难的小家伙…你的大悲大恸，是我取乐的小手段。」
t_en: "Indeed, my downtrodden fellow... Your sorrow and joy are but means for me to amuse myself."

t_ch: 我所流下的全部眼泪，都由比我更强者判定——我将为此稍感宽慰。
t_en: I will at least derive some comfort... If all the tears that I had shed were determined by some mightier being above.

t_ch: 住口…住口！
t_en: Shut up... Just shut up!

t_ch: 纵使那女孩才是邪恶，你也绝无理由为那魔物开脱。
t_en: Even if that girl is evil, there's no reason for you to absolve that monster.

t_ch: 是它们二者一道…一道将我们困在这生死的夹缝当中！
t_en: It was the both of them... The both of them who trapped us in this crevice between life and death!

t_ch: 与此同时 奥赫玛
t_en: At the same time, in Okhema

t_ch: *远方的欢呼声*
t_en: *Loud cheers from afar*

t_ch: 时光飞逝哪…转眼间，第十五个{RUBY_B#黎明}门扉时{RUBY_E#}已近在眼前了。
t_en: Time flies... In the blink of an eye, the fifteenth {RUBY_B#dawn}Entry Hour{RUBY_E#} is already at hand.

t_ch: 待到这场闹剧结束，汝之魂息也将置上生死的天平。准备好了么？
t_en: When this farce is over, your soul will be placed on the scales of life and death. Are you ready?

t_ch: 用不着这么反复确认，当然。
t_en: No need to ask so many times — of course I am.

t_ch: 那末，为何依旧踯躅不前？是汝那忌惮人群的毛病又犯了？
t_en: Then why do you drag your feet? Is your fear of crowds holding you back again?

t_ch: 只是觉得太吵了。那些愚蠢又自负的笨货，多看一眼都是浪费我的时间。
t_en: Hmph, I simply find them too noisy. Paying attention to conceited fools like them would be an utter waste of my time.

t_ch: 真是不近人情哪。依吾看，汝妄自尊大的程度也不遑多让呢。
t_en: Such harsh words. As I see it, you are no less arrogant than they are.

t_ch: 对此，我深表赞同。
t_en: I couldn't agree more.

t_ch: 哦？这反应真叫人意外，汝竟不反驳吾么？
t_en: Oh? I did not expect to hear that from you. Are you not going to refute me?

t_ch: 自己是什么样，我难道还不清楚么？至少比你这位「房客」清楚吧。
t_en: I know myself better than you think I do... At the very least, better than a stowaway like you.

t_ch: 真是人之将死，其言也不善哪。
t_en: And they say men whose ends are nigh have but kind words.

t_ch: 但也正因如此，吾才会选中了你，不是么？
t_en: Well that is exactly why I chose you.

t_ch: 哼，承蒙谬赞。
t_en: Hmph, should I thank you for the compliment?

t_ch: 所以，试炼的时刻已至，汝打算如何证明吾的问题哪？
t_en: The hour of the trial has come. Do you have an answer for me? And... how will you prove the veracity of your argument?

t_ch: 既然都到这时候了…还记得我上次讲到哪里了么？
t_en: I suppose there is no point in putting this off any longer. Do you recall where we stopped the last time?

t_ch: 汝声称：「翁法罗斯历史乃是『轮回』」——
t_en: You were saying that Amphoreus' history is cyclical...

t_ch: 没错。背负「火种」的半神，将在世界尽头成为新的泰坦……
t_en: Indeed. The demigods that bear the Coreflames eventually become the new Titans at the world's end...

t_ch: 记得在神山上的见闻吗？卡吕普索、格奈乌斯…那些英雄临到命运的尽头，次第离去，只留下递补「死亡」的双子。
t_en: Remember what happened on the holy mountain? Calypso, Gnaeus... As their destined ends approached, one by one, they left this world... leaving only the twins, who were fated to succeed Death.

t_ch: 可惜，就算他们的旅途与我们庶几相似，还有你与卡吕普索，尼卡多利与格奈乌斯从旁佐证，也只能为「轮回」的猜想勾勒蓝图。
t_en: Unfortunately, while their journey is consistent with ours — you and Calypso as well as Nikador and Gnaeus act as supporting evidence — I can only come up with a rough sketch for my theory of a cyclical history.

t_ch: 虽然大费周章走到这一步，但我对证明本来已不抱任何期望。毕竟，过去的皆已过去，要想证明我们是往日英雄的传承，无异于痴人说梦……
t_en: For all the pains I took to reach this point, I had already lost hope of ever obtaining definitive proof. After all, the past is in the past. Proving that we are the legacies of heroes of the past is a hopeless endeavor.

t_ch: 直到…我们见到了那名叫玻吕茜亚的少女和她的姐姐。
t_en: That is, until I met a girl named Polyxia and her sister.

t_ch: 遐蝶——若她果真是泰坦出于私心，从过去送到现在的生灵——那么，以斯缇科西亚为「结点」，亲身走过两个轮回的她定能成为联结一切的「纽带」。
t_en: If Castorice was truly sent from the past to the present for the Titan's self-serving reasons, then having lived through two cycles and with Styxia as the pivot, she will no doubt be the link to connect all of this together.

t_ch: 如果「死亡」的双子能够跨越两世，在冥海边重逢——我的猜想便不再是一纸空谈，你的问题也能得到解答了。
t_en: If the twins of Death can bridge two cycles to reunite at the shores of the Sea of Souls... then my hypothesis will no longer be just a hypothesis, and you, too, will get the answer to your question.

t_ch: 那敢情好。可是，汝为何能如此断定？倘若这猜想出了差池，那女孩岂不是要败兴而归？
t_en: That is all well and good, but how can you be so sure? If there is even the slightest error in your hypothesis, the girl will be shattered.

t_ch: 哼，错了又如何？身为学者，最重要的品质正是不假思索的鲁莽。如果永远谨小慎微，无法大胆迈出第一步，那一切变化也无从谈起——
t_en: Hmph, it matters not. The most important quality a scholar must possess is foolhardiness. If you are overly cautious, then you'll never have the gumption to make the first step, and change will never come.

t_ch: 更何况，我，阿那克萨戈拉斯，注定要颠覆这个有悖常理的世界。
t_en: Besides, I, Anaxagoras, am destined to overturn this farcical world.

t_ch: 走，我们去给那帮可怜虫们上最后一课。
t_en: Come, let us go and teach those pathetic worms one final lesson.

t_ch: 让他们听听吧——那些发动了残暴战争的黄金裔——！
t_en: Let those Chrysos Heirs who started this terrible war hear you!

t_ch: 让他们听见人民的呼声——！
t_en: Let them hear the voices of the people!

t_ch: *狂热的喝彩*
t_en: *fanatic cheering*

t_ch: 那女人还是一如既往，善于煽动人心。
t_en: As always, that woman knows how to stir up a crowd.

t_ch: 那刻夏…老师？我还以为你不打算到场了。
t_en: Professor Anaxa...? I thought you'd pass on this.

t_ch: 票总是要投的，否则这场大会不就毫无意义了？
t_en: Everyone must cast their vote, or this assembly would serve no purpose.

t_ch: 老师，你当真觉得…将世界的命运托付在一场大会上，是正确的么？
t_en: Prof... Do you truly think it's wise to entrust the fate of the world to an assembly like this?

t_ch: 奥赫玛在这一制度下屹立千年不倒，自然能证明其存在的合理性。
t_en: Okhema has stood strong under this system for a thousand years. It stands to reason that there is purpose in its existence.

t_ch: 但我认为，你想问的不是这个。真正令你迷茫的是…阿格莱雅将决定世界命运的机会托付给你，是否正确。
t_en: But that is not what you're asking, is it? What you really want to know is: Is Aglaea making the right decision in entrusting the fate of the world to you?

t_ch: …永远这么一针见血啊。
t_en: ...Nothing ever escapes you.

t_ch: 毕竟教了你那么久，别人可是最多四年就毕业了。
t_en: I didn't spend all those years teaching you for nothing. Most people only take four years to graduate, if I recall.

t_ch: 哈哈…坦率地讲，我感觉不像要上演讲台，反而像上刑场。
t_en: Haha... To tell the truth, I feel like I'm on my way to an execution chamber instead of a podium.

t_ch: 所以，答案是？
t_en: So... do you have an answer to that question?

t_ch: 我没法替你回答这个问题——
t_en: No, I don't.

t_ch: 也是……
t_en: I thought so...

t_ch: ——因为这问题和你没有半点关系。
t_en: Because that question is not yours to ask in the first place.

t_ch: 「阿格莱雅将机会托付给你」是否正确？别引我发笑了，这不是那女人自己的问题么？为什么你要替她考虑这些？还是说，你一直都把自己当成她的工具？
t_en: Is "Aglaea entrusting the world to you" the right decision? Don't make me laugh. That is a question that woman must answer herself. Why are you fretting about this on her behalf? Or have you been seeing yourself as her pawn all this time?

t_ch: 听好了，把思路逆转过来——「『我』要如何决定世界命运，才是正确的选择？」——这才是你真正应该思考的。
t_en: Listen up, I'll say this only once. Flip your perspective around and ask yourself this instead, "What should I do to ensure that the world doesn't stray from the correct path?" That is what you should be thinking about.

t_ch: 还有时间，好好想想你会如何作答吧，也别说给我听……
t_en: You still have time, so give it some thought. Oh, and don't bother telling me the answer.

t_ch: 去说给那群向诸神乞求拯救的人听。
t_en: Tell it to those begging for salvation from the gods.

t_ch: …明白了。
t_en: Got it.

t_ch: 谢谢你，那刻夏老师。
t_en: Thank you, Professor Anaxa.

t_ch: 叫我阿那克萨戈拉斯。
t_en: That's Anaxagoras to you.

t_ch: 你…会把票投给哪边？
t_en: Who... who'll you be voting for?

t_ch: 看你的表现…愿瑟希斯捍卫你的思想。
t_en: That'll depend on how well you perform... May Cerces safeguard your thoughts.

t_ch: 那刻夏阁下，您来了！
t_en: Lord Anaxa, you're finally here!

t_ch: 本次大会最后一名演讲人即将入场，决议将在之后举行。
t_en: The last speaker for the assembly will be taking the stage soon. The resolution will be passed after that.

t_ch: 凯妮斯大人为您留了特等席，请问要现在落座吗？
t_en: Lady Caenis has reserved a special seat for you. Shall I show you to it?

t_ch: 好，现在带路吧。
t_en: Lead the way.

t_ch: 好，我为您带路。
t_en: Alright. This way, please.

t_ch: 晚些时候再说。
t_en: Not now.

t_ch: 谨遵您吩咐。
t_en: As you command.

t_ch: 凯妮斯大人为您留了特等席，请问要现在落座吗？
t_en: Lady Caenis has reserved a special seat for you. Shall I show you to it?

t_ch: 好，现在带路吧。
t_en: Lead the way.

t_ch: 好，我为您带路。
t_en: Alright. This way, please.

t_ch: 晚些时候再说。
t_en: Not now.

t_ch: 谨遵您吩咐。
t_en: As you command.

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 元老们，公民们，请肃静。以神礼观众之名，我见到——
t_en: Elders and citizens, silence, if you please. As the Theoros, I present to you:

t_ch: ——奥赫玛正直的公民，哀丽秘榭的白厄，将为本场大会带来最后的演说！
t_en: Phainon of Aedes Elysiae, virtuous citizen of Okhema and the last speaker of the assembly.

t_ch: 感谢您，高贵的来古士阁下。
t_en: Much obliged, Lord Lygus.

t_ch: 我记得这家伙，给阿格莱雅提鞋的穷小子！
t_en: I remember him — he's the penniless lad working for Aglaea!

t_ch: 「金织」女士呢？为什么派这家伙来压台？逐火之旅真要结束了？
t_en: Where's Lady Goldweaver? Why is she sending this guy to give the last speech? Is this really the end of the Flame-Chase Journey?

t_ch: 一介武夫？这小子能镇得住凯妮斯么……
t_en: Can this dude really face down Caenis...?

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 刻法勒在上，各位奥赫玛的公民——
t_en: By Kephale above, dear citizens of Okhema...

t_ch: ——我看到了，你们所有人的目光都注视着我。
t_en: As you look upon me, so too do I see you.

t_ch: 你们的眼里饱含忧虑，不仅忧虑黄金裔与元老院之间滋生的裂隙，更忧虑这座圣城将要步入的明天。
t_en: I see your fear and misgivings, your apprehension about the discord between the Chrysos Heirs and the Council of Elders... and your uneasiness about the future of the holy city.

t_ch: 现在，我想敬请各位：在思考「世界的命运」这般宏大的命题以前，请你们先擦亮自己的双眼…越过被{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}照亮的地平线，看清我们身处的危机！
t_en: Yet, before you contemplate something as grand as the fate of the world, I ask that you look closer... Look, beyond the horizon illuminated by {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, at the crisis threatening to overwhelm us!
What...?

t_ch: 我的故乡哀丽秘榭，那是一处被时间遗忘的村落。那里的好风如薄荷般清新，麦浪宽广到能连上海洋…我曾坚信，自己会在那与邻里亲友永享安宁。
t_en: Aedes Elysiae, my beloved hometown, is a village forgotten by time. It is a place with the most invigorating breeze and wheat fields that spread all the way to the sea... I once thought that there, I would enjoy a lifetime of peace and quiet with my friends and family.

t_ch: 我多想邀请诸位前去一睹那与世隔绝的好地方啊！可我做不到——
t_en: How I wish I could invite all of you to that paradise far away from the noise and chatter of the world! Alas, I can't...

t_ch: 因为，她和她哺育的一切，我所深爱的一切，早已被黑潮吞噬——我无家可归！
t_en: ...Because she... and all that she nurtured... all that I loved... they have long been devoured by the black tide. My beloved home... is no more.

t_ch: 我永远都记得黑潮来临的那天：我的父亲希洛尼摩斯，为了他的孩子，用断剑力战到最后一刻；我的母亲奥妲塔，她为我能站在这里向诸位乞求，被怪物破开了胸膛！
t_en: I will never forget the day the black tide struck. My father, Hieronymus, fought with a broken sword to his last breath to protect his child. My mother, Audata... She was ripped apart by monsters, all so I might live to stand before you here and now.

t_ch: 猎户伽尔巴，他张弓的双臂，最终怀抱着他挚爱的幼子披索，二人双双死去；皮西厄斯，孩子们的老师，她拼命救下了所有学生，燃烧的梁木却压垮了她的脊梁……
t_en: Galba... He was a hunter. But in the end, those arms that once drew a bow instead embraced Piso, his beloved son, as they both breathed their last. Pythias, the teacher, risked her life to save her students, but a burning beam fell on her, crushing her...

t_ch: 可她的女儿莉维娅呢？她逃出生天了吗？没有：她、披索，还有其他许多孩子，我童年时最亲密的玩伴们…他们全都被黑潮吞没，变成了扭曲的造物！
t_en: And what of Livia, her daughter? Did she make it out alive? ...No, she didn't. Livia, Piso, and so many other children... My childhood friends... They were all swallowed by the black tide and turned into monsters.

t_ch: 直到我下定决心向他们挥剑，为他们带去体面的死亡，他们还在对我喃喃道——
t_en: They kept saying the same thing over and over... even up until the final moment I finally steeled my heart and granted them eternal peace with my sword:

t_ch: 「你怎么了，白厄？难道我们不是最好的朋友吗？」
t_en: "Why, Phainon? Aren't we the best of friends?"

t_ch: 这就是黑潮，奥赫玛如今迫在眉睫的威胁。凯妮斯或许承诺了，灾厄三泰坦皆已陨落，奥赫玛必将重返「黄金世」——
t_en: That is the black tide — a looming threat that can bring Okhema to its knees anytime. Caenis may have promised that, with the Three Titans of Calamity gone, Okhema will return to an Era Chrysea once more, but...

t_ch: 但现在，我要让各位失望了。因为，我会对你们说：即便{RUBY_B#万敌}「纷争」之半神{RUBY_E#}已为我们挡下许多，但「它」就要来了。
t_en: Much as it pains me, I must disappoint you: While {RUBY_B#Mydei}the demigod of Strife{RUBY_E#} has done everything in his power to hold them back, "they" are still coming.

t_ch: 那黑袍剑士…「盗火行者」的名字，恐怕早已传遍大街小巷。现在，「纷争」用他的惊雷送来信号，告诉我们：它已自死灰中复活，并渴求着众神的火种。
t_en: By now, the name of that black-robed swordmaster... the Flame Reaver, has likely echoed throughout the city already. The roaring thunder is a message from the Strife demigod: The Flame Reaver has risen from death, and they hunger for the Coreflames of the gods.

t_ch: 扯谎！如果这么恐怖的敌人近在眼前，奥赫玛岂能继续享受眼前的和平？
t_en: Lies! If such a powerful enemy is indeed approaching, why is Okhema still a picture of peace?

t_ch: 正是！奥赫玛美好的公民生活没有停歇的迹象。仔细想想吧，各位，这场公民大会能够和平召开，难道是因为危机消失了吗？
t_en: Exactly! The city of Okhema shows no signs of pandemonium or chaos. And why is that? I beg you, spare a moment to think about this. Are we able to stand here and hold this assembly in peace today because the danger has passed?

t_ch: 不！那是因为黄金裔们挺身而出，将灾厄挡在了奥赫玛的城墙之外——阿格莱雅用她的金线，维系并捍卫了你们所能享用的一切！
t_en: No! This peace we enjoy is no gift of fate. We are able to stand here today only because the Chrysos Heirs have stepped up and shielded Okhema from calamity! With her golden threads, Aglaea has preserved — and continues to preserve — these idyllic days that all of you hold dear!
Heh...

t_ch: 元老院说，她会抛下所有人，只因神性正在蚕食她的身心。但各位可曾想过，那些贵人本能向她伸出援手——他们为何选择了冷眼旁观，并将奥赫玛推离逐火的事业？
t_en: The Council of Elders claims that Aglaea will one day forsake the people because her divinity is eroding her body and soul. But ask yourselves this: Rather than offering her aid and support, why do they choose to watch and push Okhema away from the Flame-Chase Journey?

t_ch: 那无人能说得出口的事实…那血淋淋的，被贪婪、恐惧和虚荣所埋没的真相，就由我来掘地三尺，将它呈现在你们眼前吧。
t_en: The answer that no one can bring themselves to speak of... A truth stained in blood and buried in greed, fear, and vanity... Allow me to lay it bare before you.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>承载着奥赫玛悠久历史和威严的元老院，我们曾引以为豪的公民大会——如今已经遍地虫豸！</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>It is because the Council of Elders, which bears Okhema's history and honor, along with this citizens' assembly we once took so much pride in, are now infested with vermin!</color>

t_ch: 放肆！你怎么敢在圣地——
t_en: You insolent whelp! How dare you—

t_ch: 对！阿格莱雅现在独木难支了——因为，在虫豸们于光天化日下望风捕影、酝酿阴谋之时，她正呕心沥血地编织圣城的防御网，以对抗世间无孔不入的恶毒！
t_en: It is true that Aglaea has reached her limit. Why? Because she has been working tirelessly to weave the defenses of our holy city against the encroaching evil — all while these vermin are making up baseless accusations about her and scheming in the shadows!

t_ch: 她从未辜负奥赫玛的期待，却只因虫豸的围攻撕咬、就该蒙受莫大的羞辱吗？除却那后至的「神性」…她的本心，难道不是始终与「人」同在吗？
t_en: Not once has she ever failed Okhema, yet these vermin attempt to tear her apart, tarnishing her name with unfounded allegations! She may have been blessed by divinity, but hasn't her heart always stayed true to humanity?

t_ch: 他们想欺骗你们，把这场大会粗暴地归结成「人」与「神」的对立。还记得我的请求吗？奥赫玛的公民们，请擦亮双眼，仔细端详一下站在你们身边的人吧！
t_en: They attempt to mislead you and reduce this assembly to a crude standoff between gods and humans, but citizens of Okhema! Once more, I implore you to open your eyes and look at those around you!

t_ch: 看得再仔细些！看看被那些华贵的衣袍所遮盖的，是人的热肠？还是害虫的毒腺？
t_en: Examine them closely! Beneath all that finery, do you see the good heart of a human... or the poisonous glands of a pest?

t_ch: 待你们在心中下完了判断，再把目光投向她们吧——看看那你们以为冷漠的半神，是否还闪耀着人性的光；而那披着人皮恶语中伤的，又是否着了权欲的魔！
t_en: Make your judgment, then turn your gaze to the demigods whom so many of you regard as cold and merciless — do you not see the light of humanity in them? And those who wear the mask of humanity yet utter only hurtful, slanderous words — can you not see that their souls have been corrupted by power?

t_ch: 但无论你们得出了什么样的答案，最终将手中的陶片投给了谁…你们都不必有后顾之忧。
t_en: Yet, no matter what your answer is, and no matter who your vote goes to... rest assured that you have nothing to fear.

t_ch: 因为我、阿格莱雅、缇里西庇俄丝，我们仍会是你们忠诚的护墙，誓死捍卫你们所有人的抉择——
t_en: For Aglaea, Tribios, and I will remain your faithful guardians and pledge our lives to upholding your decision.

t_ch: 如此承诺，将一直延续至我们生命的终点，并被传递给未来的逐火之人——
t_en: And this vow will stay true until the end of our days, where it will be passed on to future Flame-Chasers...

t_ch: 万世不息，永不动摇！
t_en: ...A steadfast and unwavering promise that shall endure through the ages!

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 在一片哗然中，奥赫玛不同城邦代表领袖将手中的陶片扔进陶罐中……
t_en: In an uproar, representative leaders from different cities in Okhema throw pieces of pottery into the dolium...

t_ch: 截至目前，「继续逐火之旅」者与「暂停逐火之旅」者，双方民意在{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的天平上……
t_en: At the present time, on the scales of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, those who call for the continuation of the Flame-Chase Journey and those who demand its suspension...

t_ch: 票数相等，形成了绝佳的平衡。
t_en: Each faction holds an equal share of the votes, resulting in the perfect balance.

t_ch: 不出所料，这是逐火之旅距离「中止」最近的一刻。
t_en: As expected, this is the moment when the Flame-Chase Journey is the closest to its end.

t_ch: 现在，轮到您了，阿那克萨戈拉斯阁下。
t_en: And now, it's your turn to vote, Lord Anaxagoras.

t_ch: 还有其他人没有投票么？
t_en: Is there anyone else who hasn't voted?

t_ch: 您是最后一位。慎重思考吧，您的庄严一票将打破这场完美的僵局。
t_en: No, you're the last. Cast your vote with care, for your decision will break this impasse.

t_ch: 我早有把握。
t_en: I have long since made a decision.

t_ch: 甚好。衷心祝愿您为这有悖常理的世界带来真正的变革。
t_en: Very well. May you bring much-needed change to this farcical world.

t_ch: 你干得很好，白厄。在那种形势下，用激烈的言论激发起情绪是一步妙招。
t_en: You've done well, Phainon. Given the circumstances, delivering a rousing speech to stir up emotions was a prudent move.

t_ch: 感谢诸位耐心等待。我，阿那克萨戈拉斯，谨代表神悟树庭七贤人与全体学者，投下庄严一票。
t_en: Thank you all for your patience. On behalf of the Seven Sages of the Grove of Epiphany and the scholars of Amphoreus, I, Anaxagoras, am ready to cast my vote.

t_ch: 不过为了公平起见，「金织」女士，我会在所有人面前向你提问——
t_en: But before that, in the interest of fairness, Lady Goldweaver, I pose a question to you before all who are gathered here:

t_ch: 请你告诉我：你确实如白厄所说，无论结果如何，都将捍卫本次大会庄严的决议么？
t_en: Will you, as Phainon has promised, stand by the decision made in the assembly, regardless of outcome?

t_ch: 为公平起见…我不会向你做出任何承诺，以免干扰你的判断。
t_en: In the interest of fairness... I make no promises, lest it should influence your decision.

t_ch: 呵，那敢情好。感谢您的肺腑之言。
t_en: Heh, very well. I thank you for your honesty.

t_ch: 这家伙干嘛呢？
t_en: What's he doing?

t_ch: 事关重大，给他一点时间吧。
t_en: The stakes are high. Let's be patient.

t_ch: 别摆谱了！你以为自己是什么东西，要我们陪你在这儿浪费时间？
t_en: Cut the act! Who do you think you are, wasting our time like this?

t_ch: 那刻夏阁下，别忘了……
t_en: Lord Anaxa, please do not forget that I—

t_ch: 第一，别叫我那刻夏；第二，别打断我——沉默是金。
t_en: Rule number one: Do not call me Anaxa. Rule number two: Never interrupt me. Silence is golden.

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 投票啊！还愣着干嘛？……
t_en: Just vote already! What are you waiting for?
Cast... your... vote...

t_ch: 周围的声音逐渐被滤去，那刻夏神识中{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的声音逐渐清晰。
t_en: The surrounding sounds gradually fade away, and the voice of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} in Anaxa's mind becomes increasingly clear.

t_ch: 吾不禁在想，是否世间一切磨难都会被汝当作物尽其用的手段。
t_en: I cannot help but wonder: Is every hardship in the world something you will exploit and use to your advantage?

t_ch: 呵呵，身体和灵魂一分为二，不就该这么用吗？
t_en: Heh, how else am I supposed to make use of this situation where my body and soul are separated?

t_ch: 吾师瑟希斯，亦或卡吕普索，最后陪我走一遭吧——
t_en: O Cerces — or should I say, Calypso — accompany me on this final journey.

t_ch: 走，到冥界去——去看看她将如何给你的提问、我的抉择，带来完美的证明。
t_en: Let us journey to the nether realm and see how she will substantiate the answer to your question and validate my decision.

t_ch: 只要，循着潮声……
t_en: We need only to follow the sound of the tides...

t_ch: 你是……
t_en: You... you are...

t_ch: 很好，孩子…我早就说过，你会大有作为的。
t_en: Well done, child. Just as I foresaw, you have achieved great things.

t_ch: 吾师……
t_en: Teacher...

t_ch: 真是奇妙的际遇啊…「敬拜学派」教出了一名渎神者，简直就像是小毛驴，吸干了妈妈的奶，反倒踢了妈妈一脚。
t_en: What a twist of fate... A blasphemer raised in the doctrines of the Venerationists. It's like a foal kicking its mother in the belly after taking all her milk.

t_ch: 我敬爱你，老师。但你明白……
t_en: I respect you immensely, sir, but—

t_ch: 你更加爱真理。
t_en: You cherish the truth even more.

t_ch: 所以，孩子，不必为我所承受的非议感到遗憾。别让那些噪音掣住你前进的步伐。
t_en: So, child, do not be troubled by the criticisms I have borne. Do not let those voices hinder you as you forge ahead.

t_ch: 作为「敬拜学派」的贤人，世俗要求我不能认同你；但是，作为阿那克萨戈拉斯的友人与导师，我会说……
t_en: As the Sage of the Venerationists, I am duty-bound to oppose you. But as your friend and mentor, Anaxagoras, I say:

t_ch: 「去做吧，真理已尽在你手中了。」
t_en: Go forth, for the truth is already in your hands.

t_ch: …我会铭记于心。
t_en: ...I will keep that in mind.

t_ch: 好了，前进吧，孩子。你孤独的求索路即将迎来新的起点——
t_en: Go now, child. Your lonely pursuit is about to welcome a new chapter...

t_ch: 而当你抵达其尽头时，愿我们在真理前相会。
t_en: And when you reach the end of this journey, may we meet again with the truth before us.

t_ch: 我向你保证，老师。愿瑟希斯捍卫你的思想。
t_en: You have my word, sir. May Cerces safeguard your thoughts.

t_ch: 愿瑟希斯捍卫你的思想。
t_en: May Cerces safeguard your thoughts.

t_ch: 你说得对，吾师。我孤独的求索路即将迎来新的起点，我得出发了——
t_en: You are right, Empedocles. My lonely pursuit is about to welcome a new chapter. I must be on my way now...

t_ch: ——愿我们在真理前相会，吾师。
t_en: May we meet again with the truth before us, Empedocles.

t_ch: 你还在等什么，快投票——
t_en: What are you waiting for? Just cast your vote—

t_ch: 嘘——都安静——！
t_en: Silence! Silence, everyone!

t_ch: 看吧，{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}——此刻，在世界的中心，我将写下最后一步证明……
t_en: Behold, {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}. Here at the center of the world, I shall write the final step of my proof...

t_ch: ——此处距离真理，只有一步之遥。
t_en: For this place is but one step away from the truth.

t_ch: 平衡已被打破，结果已经明朗。{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}在上，以神礼观众之名，我见到——
t_en: With the balance shattered, the result is clear. By {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} above, as the Theoros, I have observed...

t_ch: 不…来古士，等等…！
t_en: No! Lygus, you can't...!

t_ch: 那刻夏…这是在做什么？你疯了。
t_en: Anaxa! What have you done!? Have you lost your mind!?

t_ch: 我会准备好与你相称的酷刑…而你得用那颗聪明脑袋好好记住，什么是背叛的代价…！
t_en: I hope you are prepared for the harsh punishment awaiting you! Mark my words, I will make sure that clever head of yours never forgets the price of betrayal!

t_ch: 还记得吗？我说过，这恰恰是我最宝贵的天赋之一。
t_en: Have you forgotten? I told you, that happens to be one of my rare talents.

t_ch: 你不惜演到这份上…就为了帮助他们完成愚蠢的逐火之旅…？！
t_en: You put on such a grand performance... just to aid their preposterous Flame-Chase Journey!?

t_ch: 「演」？错了，即便生性多疑，我也从来以直诚闻名。
t_en: Performance? No, you are quite mistaken. I may be paranoid by nature, but I've always been known for my honesty.

t_ch: 是你误会了，凯妮斯阁下。你不了解我，我从不关心你们那可怜的政治游戏。什么阵营、立场，无一例外，全都是笑话。我的选择从未变过……
t_en: If you really understood me, Lady Caenis, you'd realize that I never had any interest in your pathetic little game of politics. Factions, standpoints... they are jokes in my eyes. I have never once switched sides...

t_ch: 只有我自己，才是唯一确凿的真理。
t_en: Because I, and I alone, am the truth.

t_ch: 凯妮斯阁下，在公民目光的注视下，我们应坚持民主的精神，尊重议会结果。
t_en: Lady Caenis, with all the citizens here as witnesses, we should honor the spirit of democracy and respect the decision of this assembly.

t_ch: 我见到——奥赫玛已选择了她的命运。公民大会决定继续支持逐火之旅！该决议并将张贴于纪名英雄墙前，全体公民应知晓城邦的前路。
t_en: ...I have observed that Okhema has chosen her destiny! This citizens' assembly has resolved to continue the Flame-Chase Journey. This resolution shall be put up on the Wall of Heroes such that all citizens are aware of the city's way forward.
You...!

t_ch: 看来有人还不尽兴啊。那好，不妨让我来为这场大会再添一笔吧。
t_en: It seems not everyone is ready for the assembly to end. Very well, let me entertain you for a little longer.

t_ch: 来古士阁下，可以么？
t_en: By your leave, Lord Lygus.

t_ch: 有趣。
t_en: Interesting...

t_ch: 元老们，公民们，请肃静！神悟树庭的阿那克萨戈拉斯请求向公民大会宣告。还请各位留步，以便聆听其宣言。
t_en: Elders and citizens, your attention, please! Stay for a moment more, if you will. Anaxagoras of the Grove of Epiphany has an announcement for the assembly.

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 诸君！就在刚才，你们亲手决定了翁法罗斯的命运。作为逐火之旅的支持者，我也十分乐见诸位的抉择——
t_en: Just moments ago, all of you made a decision on the fate of Amphoreus. As a supporter of the Flame-Chase Journey, it pleases me to see the choice you have made.

t_ch: 但是，很遗憾：我仍然对此结果深表质疑，并要求挑战此次决议！
t_en: But I regret to say that I still harbor doubts about this outcome. Thus, I hereby challenge this resolution!
Huh...

t_ch: 告诉我！在座各位，有多少人敢对{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}起誓，「再创世」的尽头必将如诸位黄金裔所说，是一片没有灾厄与疯狂的美丽新世界？
t_en: Tell me, who among you dares swear on {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s name that Era Nova will truly welcome the fairy-tale ending that the Chrysos Heirs have promised? A beautiful new world free of calamity and madness?

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 为什么没人敢举手回答？非要我点名不成？
t_en: No one? Must I pick somebody to answer?

t_ch: 那好。那边故作深沉的家伙，你敢对在场所有人赌咒发誓吗？
t_en: Very well. You, the one acting so wise and profound — in front of all present here, do you dare swear that it will happen for sure?

t_ch: 不能？下一位。那个一脸蠢相的，对，就是你，你敢对着泰坦拍胸脯做担保吗？
t_en: No? Fine, next. You, the one with ignorance written all over your face... Yes, you. Do you dare swear by it in front of the Titan?

t_ch: 这是在干嘛？
t_en: What are you trying to do?

t_ch: 哼，废物！那换你来，看你鼻子早就翘到天上去了。这么有能耐，你敢保证逐火之旅是最好的选择吗？
t_en: Hmph, useless! Your turn, then. With that smug look of yours, you must be confident that the Flame-Chase Journey is the best course to take, right?

t_ch: 你小子…骂谁呢！
t_en: Hey! Mind your tongue!

t_ch: 都答不上来？行吧。白厄，既然是你慷慨陈词，那我倒要问问：你心里又有几成把握？
t_en: So, no one? I see. In that case, Phainon, answer me this: Your speech was filled with such passion just now. How confident, then, are you in making this promise?

t_ch: 那刻夏老师！你究竟在干什么……
t_en: Professor! What are you doing?

t_ch: …坐下。
t_en: ...Sit down.

t_ch: 让他…继续表演吧。
t_en: Let him continue with his performance.

t_ch: 哈，看啊！连你白厄和阿格莱雅都没这个胆量！
t_en: Haha! Will you look at that? Even Phainon and Aglaea lack the courage to make such a promise!

t_ch: 更荒谬的是，除了我，根本没人能承诺这道美好的愿景！可大会竟在无知中贸然通过了这一决议？
t_en: And what's even more absurd is that no one but I can promise you this new world, and yet the assembly passed the resolution anyway!

t_ch: 不过，诸位不必担心，还好我对你们这帮酒囊饭袋早就心里有数。所以我才会出现在这里，来给你们所有人上最后一课！
t_en: But you needn't worry because I knew better than to trust you good-for-nothings. That is precisely why I am here today — to give you one final lecture!

t_ch: 都给我提起耳朵，听清楚了！现在，我，神悟树庭的阿那克萨戈拉斯，就这样告诉你们——
t_en: Now, listen up, and listen well! I, Anaxagoras of the Grove of Epiphany, hereby declare:

t_ch: 逐火之旅许诺的一切确凿无疑：因为预言中{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的半神，哀丽秘榭的白厄，将以他完整无缺的记忆，将我们所有人重塑在新世界中！
t_en: Everything that the Flame-Chase Journey promises is true! Phainon of Aedes Elysiae, the prophesized demigod who shall succeed {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, will reforge each and every one of us in the new world with his complete, intact memories!

t_ch: 什么…？
t_en: What...?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>没错，身负火种的泰坦即为过去的黄金裔，背负同样火种的黄金裔正是未来的泰坦——</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The Coreflame-bearing Titans were the Chrysos Heirs of yesterday, and the Chrysos Heirs who carry the Coreflames today will become the Titans of tomorrow.</color>

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>而我们所有人的灵魂，都将在负世的神迹下永存不灭——我承诺，刻法勒庇护下的一切，都必将步入那新生的世界！</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>All of our souls shall endure for eternity in the miracle of the Worldbearer, and all under Kephale's protection shall step into that new world!</color>

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 什么…就算到了来世，我们都要对你们顶礼膜拜…？！
t_en: What!? Are you saying that we must bow down to you and worship you even in our next lives!?

t_ch: 住口！你这渎神的狗鼠，休想妖言惑众！你有什么根据……
t_en: Enough, you blasphemous imbecile! How dare you try to deceive us with all these lies and falsehoods! How can you prove—

t_ch: 我说过，别打断我！但既然你问了，那就回答你吧：我的根据就来自对{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}神体的实验——
t_en: I told you, don't interrupt me! But since you've asked, I shall be kind and indulge you: My statements are based on my experiments on the divine body of {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} themself!

t_ch: 没错，诸君！在凯妮斯与元老院全体的默许授意下，我以炼金之法将自己的灵魂与尊神熔合，由此揭示了有关「再创世」的一切！
t_en: You heard me correctly, everyone! With the tacit approval of Caenis and the Council of Elders, I fused my soul with a god through alchemy and learned the truth behind Era Nova!

t_ch: 他在说…什么东西？
t_en: What is he talking about...?

t_ch: 这…不是天大的亵渎吗？
t_en: Isn't that pure blasphemy?

t_ch: 我，卑劣可耻的渎神者，应受死刑制裁……
t_en: I, the vile, wretched blasphemer, deserve nothing less than death for my sins...

t_ch: 而包庇我、纵容我，协助我完成渎神罪行的元老院，应与我同罪！
t_en: And the Council of Elders, which granted me consent and aided me in committing this atrocity, should face punishment alongside me!

t_ch: 天啊，连凯妮斯也背叛了奥赫玛……
t_en: By the gods, even Caenis has betrayed Okhema...

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 我对于罪人的所作所为概、概不知情…他的一切罪行皆与元老院无关…！
t_en: I—I had no knowledge of his transgressions...! He did that on his own! The Council of Elders bears no responsibility for his actions!

t_ch: 来古士阁下…你身为最高贵的神礼观众，听到元老院遭到如此污蔑，还打算无动于衷吗…？！
t_en: Lord Lygus... As the esteemed Theoros, are you going to simply stand by and allow the Council of Elders to be dragged through the mud!?

t_ch: 如果渎职属实，我会大方承认；如果没有，我便不怀担忧。凯妮斯阁下，元老院的信誉并非在朝夕之间建立，也不会在朝夕之间崩溃。
t_en: If the accusation is true, I will acknowledge it. If not, my conscience stands unburdened. Lady Caenis, the reputation of the Council of Elders was not built in a day, nor will it be shattered overnight.

t_ch: 愚弄人民者必得报应，弃誓者必得酷刑。我们应当尊重这座美丽的城邦和她的人民。
t_en: Repercussions await those who fool the people, and punishment must be meted out to those who abandon their vows. We should respect this beautiful city and her people.
You...

t_ch: 「金织」阿格莱雅女士，我请求您秉承{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}之公平，{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}之正义，主持判决——
t_en: Lady Aglaea, the Goldweaver, I humbly ask that you take on the responsibility of overseeing this judgment, in the name of the fairness of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} and the justice of {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: ——基于眼下状况，根据奥赫玛法典，在座诸位中只有身为「半神」的您享有该权利。
t_en: ...Considering the circumstances, and in accordance with Okhema's Codex, as the only demigod among us, you shall rightfully wield this authority.

t_ch: …我深感荣幸。
t_en: ...The honor is mine to bear.

t_ch: *观众议论纷纷*
t_en: *crowd chatter*

t_ch: 元老们，公民们，请肃静！
t_en: Elders and citizens, silence, if you please!

t_ch: 神悟树庭的阿那克萨戈拉斯，其陈词诸位皆已明了。现在，根据奥赫玛法典，我要求诸位公民与我共同行使审判的权力。
t_en: Anaxagoras of the Grove of Epiphany has made his statement clear. As per Okhema's Codex, I request all present citizens to stand with me in exercising the right of judgment.

t_ch: 首先，对于奥赫玛元老凯妮斯，与「神礼观众」来古士，被指控疏忽职守，徇私枉法，致使尊神奥体遭受严重亵渎……
t_en: First, Elder Caenis of Okhema and Lygus the Theoros stand accused of neglecting their duties and abusing the law, leading to the egregious blasphemy against a god...

t_ch: 以{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}之名：奥赫玛应在第七个{RUBY_B#午时}践行时{RUBY_E#}以前责成委员会展开检察，并公开宣示结果，以便进一步审理！
t_en: In the names of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, Okhema is to form a committee before the seventh {RUBY_B#Noon}Action Hour{RUBY_E#} to conduct an investigation and disclose the findings to the public for subsequent judgment!

t_ch: 赞成！赞成！
t_en: Agreed! Agreed!

t_ch: 该死…！
t_en: Curse you...!

t_ch: 其次，对于神悟树庭的阿那克萨戈拉斯，指控其自身对{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的严重亵渎——
t_en: Now, concerning Anaxagoras of the Grove of Epiphany, he has confessed to grave blasphemy against {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 以{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}之名：死刑执行前，受刑者应有权利听从「一致欢呼」，以便争取从轻发落及赦免的权利！
t_en: In the names of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, the accused must be allowed to hear the "people's voice" prior to the execution for a chance at a reduced sentence and possible pardon!

t_ch: 死刑！死刑！
t_en: Let him die! Let him die!

t_ch: 死刑！死刑！
t_en: Let him die! Let him die!

t_ch: 老师……
t_en: Professor...

t_ch: 死刑！死刑！
t_en: Let him die! Let him die!

t_ch: 死刑！死刑！
t_en: Let him die! Let him die!

t_ch: 您会如何选择呢，「金织」女士？
t_en: What decision will you make, Lady Goldweaver?

t_ch: 死刑！死刑！
t_en: Let him die! Let him die!

t_ch: 别犹豫了，阿格莱雅。这可不是你的作风。
t_en: Enough with the hesitation, Aglaea. This is not like you.

t_ch: 死刑！死刑！死刑！死刑！
t_en: Let him die! Let him die! Let him die! Let him die!

t_ch: 那么，依据「一致欢呼」结果……
t_en: Then, in light of the "people's voice"...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}在上，愿你为他带来「死亡」的命运。
t_en: O {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#} on high, may you bring death upon him.

t_ch: *狂热的喝彩*
t_en: *fanatic cheering*

t_ch: 白厄…最后一辩交给他了啊。
t_en: Phainon... So, they are leaving the final debate to him.

t_ch: 别忘了你我共同筹划的愿景，阁下。
t_en: I trust you haven't forgotten the world we've envisioned together.
Heh...

t_ch: 最后一票…
t_en: The last vote...

t_ch: 他会投给谁？
t_en: Who will he vote for?

t_ch: 哼，还是这么爱演。
t_en: Hmph, always the performer.

t_ch: 排场真大…
t_en: Talk about a grand entrance...

t_ch: 投给凯妮斯！
t_en: Vote for Caenis!

t_ch: 大局已定了…
t_en: The die is cast...
(A sea of flowers...?)

t_ch: （这是…什么地方？）
t_en: (What... is this place?)

t_ch: 阁下，来重新认识一下吧：我在人间的名字叫作遐蝶，如今是「死亡」之半神，塞纳托斯的生之侧面……
t_en: Allow me to reintroduce myself: I bore the name of Castorice while walking among the mortals, and now I stand as the demigod of Death, the living side of Thanatos...

t_ch: 欢迎来到西风尽头，我的冥界。
t_en: Welcome to my realm, where the west wind ends.

t_ch: 你的冥界很美。
t_en: It's a beautiful place.

t_ch: 每一位来到这里的亡者，都会发出同样的感叹。
t_en: Every departed soul who has arrived here has expressed the same.

t_ch: 妈妈，我终于杀青了！
t_en: Mom, I made it! My character arc is finally done!

t_ch: 你还是这么有活力。
t_en: You're still as energetic as always.

t_ch: 你知道吗，阁下？这片花海，此地的每一朵鲜花，都由塞纳托斯亲手栽下。它们本来无生无灭，但在冥河带来的第一场雨中，它们获得新生，承载着无数灵魂发荣滋长……
t_en: Did you know? Every flower here was planted by Thanatos themself. These flowers were originally both lifeless and immortal, but in the first rain brought by the River of Souls, they were given new life, flourishing as the bearers of countless souls...

t_ch: 身为「死亡」的主人，我理应悉心照料，令它们在这片恬美的天地发芽绽放。
t_en: And it is my duty as the master of Death to tend to their well-being and foster their growth in this serene haven.

t_ch: 正如凡间死生有序，这些花也会在成长的尽头枯萎、凋零，化作泥土，乘着雾气和海潮，升入天空……
t_en: Similar to the structured cycle of life and death in the mortal world, these flowers too shall wither and fade, returning to the earth and then ascending to the sky with mist and tides...

t_ch: 最后，它们会变成无声的细雨，落向人间大地。同一颗灵魂将以不同的面貌轮回流转，直至世界尽头……
t_en: Eventually, they will descend to the mortal land as gentle rain. Every soul undergoes the perpetual cycle of reincarnation, albeit in varied forms, until the world meets its end...

t_ch: 可惜，翁法罗斯已经等不到下一次鲜花绽放了。
t_en: Yet, sadly, there's no time left for Amphoreus to wait for when the flowers bloom again.

t_ch: 现在，「死亡」已是我指尖的涟漪，它曾引渡世间的亡魂来到这里，但我——要洞开冥府的大门，令你回到生者的世界……
t_en: Now, Death is the power at my fingertips. It once led the souls of the deceased here, but I... I shall open the gate of the netherworld and return you to the realm of the living...

t_ch: 作为交换…请你带回「死亡」的火种，完成逐火之旅，别让我们至今的努力付诸东流。
t_en: In exchange... Please, take the Coreflame of Death with you and complete the Flame-Chase Journey. Do not let our work thus far be in vain.

t_ch: 我向你保证。
t_en: You have my word.

t_ch: 谢谢你。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 但，你不回去了么…
t_en: But you're not going back...

t_ch: 你…不回去了么？
t_en: You're... not going back?

t_ch: 代价是什么？
t_en: But at what cost?

t_ch: 放心，「拒绝死亡」本来就是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的神权。幸好，阁下的灵魂还未消散，这并非死而复生，而是引渡一位垂死之人回到现世。
t_en: Rest assured, "defying death" is {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' divine authority. Fortunately, your soul hasn't dissipated. This is not a resurrection, but the extradition of a dying person back to the present world.

t_ch: 那，你不回去了么…
t_en: So you're not going back...

t_ch: 嗯，就像其他半神一样，我也要留在这里…拥抱自己的命运了。
t_en: Mm, just like the other demigods, I will stay here... and embrace my destiny.

t_ch: 只是，指引一位迷途者重返人间绝非易举。死亡的呢喃甘美而宁静，倘若意志不足，必将被冥府的长臂死死牵制。
t_en: That being said, it is not easy to guide a lost soul back to the mortal realm. The whispers of Death are sweet and alluring. If your determination wavers, you will succumb and be forever ensnared by the netherworld's grasp.

t_ch: 请把这当作一次特例中的特例，我会尽力而为，为了逐火之旅……
t_en: Please consider this a one-time exception. I will do everything in my power for the Flame-Chase Journey...

t_ch: …也为了此生，我唯一能拯救的生命。
t_en: ...And for your life, the only life I can save.

t_ch: 我会竭尽全力。
t_en: I'll do my best.

t_ch: 那真是…太好了。
t_en: I am... glad to hear that.

t_ch: 别了，阁下。愿我们…在新世界再会。
t_en: Farewell, then. May we... reunite in the new world.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，千万别回头啊。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, don't look back.

t_ch: 生死有命，但不是现在。
t_en: Life and death are predetermined, but yours life doesn't end here.

t_ch: 迷路了吗？我们都在前面。
t_en: Are you lost? We're all waiting for you ahead.

t_ch: 别忘记「开拓」，让它成为你的指引。
t_en: Remember the Trailblaze. Let it guide your way.

t_ch: 听我说：那里不是家。
t_en: Listen, your home is not there.

t_ch: 迷茫的时候，就寻找流星落下的方向吧。
t_en: Look for the direction of the falling stars when you're lost.

t_ch: 别回头，别让遐蝶后悔。
t_en: Don't look back. Don't make Castorice regret this.
